omma.heroDesc: The first AI system that learns from your state — without asking, without forms, without profiles.
omma.card0Desc: OMMΩ reflects your affective state through how you interact — speed, rhythm, hesitations. Not what you write, but how you write it.
omma.card1Desc: Each session refines the model. Over time, the system converges to a unique representation of you, not a generic profile.
omma.card2Desc: The final product will process locally. The current research phase collects data with your explicit documented consent — read the full terms.
omma.quoteText: We built OMMΩ so that in 10 years no AI system needs to ask how you're doing. It will already know by the way you interact with it.
omma.ctaTitle: Be the first to use OMMΩ
omma.ctaSubtitle: Early access via research participation. Beta expected 2027.

footer.tagline: The first mind that sees yours.

study.metaDesc: How OMMΩ learns from your somatic typing pattern — IKI, privacy, methodology, and how to participate.
study.heroTitle: How OMMΩ learns from your keyboard
study.heroDesc: Research on Inter-Keystroke Interval (IKI) — the somatic rhythm of your typing — and how it may be the most honest interface between humans and AI.
study.sec1Title: What is IKI and why it matters
study.sec1p1: Inter-Keystroke Interval is the time — in milliseconds — between each key you press when typing. Not what you write, but the rhythm with which you write it.
study.sec1p2: This rhythm varies with your internal state: when you're focused, the pattern differs from when you're anxious, tired, or in a flow state. IKI is, in a sense, an involuntary somatic signal — a window into the physiological state normally hidden behind text.
study.sec1p3: The central hypothesis of the research, supported by James Russell's Circumplex Model of Affect, Rosalind Picard's Affective Computing tradition, and António Damásio's Somatic Marker Hypothesis as philosophical motivation, is that this pattern can be captured, normalized, and used as context in AI systems — without any content or identity data.
study.sec2p1: You receive a narrative prompt — a question about a difficult decision you made recently — and write freely in response. While you type, the system records the time between each key (IKI) and, every 200 characters, asks two quick questions about your emotional state in the moment: valence (positive/negative) and arousal (calm/agitated).
study.sec2p2: The text you write, the keyboard events with their timestamps, and your emotional responses are sent and stored on our servers. IKI is calculated from these events. Participation takes 8 to 15 minutes and requires a physical keyboard.
study.sec3Title: What we collect — and what we don't
study.sec3Collect: We collect: the text you write during the session, keyboard events (key code and timestamp), your mood slider responses, typing speed (WPM), and at the end of the first session, IP and user-agent for duplicate control.
study.sec3NotCollect: We don't collect: clipboard content, browser history, other open applications, audio or video. Data is not sold or shared. Your participant code is stored as a SHA-256 hash — there is no direct link to your name or identity outside the research context.
study.sec4p1: Access to the waitlist is exclusive to those who completed Session 1 of the research. If you participated and arrived here via the link sent by Xcorphion, your email can be registered below.
study.ineligibleNoCode: To join the list, access this page via the link sent at the end of Session 1 of the research.

welcome.title: All set.
welcome.desc: Your profile has been created. Session 1 is now unlocked — you will write freely in response to a question about a decision you made. The rhythm of your keystrokes is the data that matters.
welcome.codeNote: Save this code — it's your access for all sessions.

convite.blockedDesc: Please contact the person who invited you to the research.
convite.errorDesc: Please check the link you received or contact the person who invited you to the research.
convite.doneDesc: {name}, your participation has been successfully completed.
convite.waitingDesc: {name}, you don't need to do anything right now. When the next session is released, this link will continue to work.
convite.codeNote: Save this code — it's your access for all sessions.
convite.session1Title: You have been invited to participate in a research study.
convite.session1Body0: {name}, {referrer} referred you to an academic study on how people write when making important decisions.
convite.session1Body1: It is not a test. There are no right or wrong answers.
convite.session1Body2: You will write freely about a real decision you experienced — and while you write, the rhythm of your keystrokes will be recorded anonymously. Nothing more.
convite.session1Body3: This is Session 1 of 3. Each session takes between 8 and 15 minutes and can be done on different days.
convite.session2Body0: {name}, thank you for completing the first session.
convite.session2Body1: {referrer} believed you would have something valuable to contribute — and you confirmed that.
convite.session2Body2: This is Session 2 of 3. The format is identical to the first: write freely, without pressure, in a quiet environment.
convite.session3Body1: This research only exists because people like you, referred by {referrer}, chose to dedicate time to something still being built.
convite.session3Body2: After this session, your participation will be complete. The data collected — always anonymous — will feed a model trying to understand how the body responds while the mind decides.

iki.completedDesc: You have already completed all available sessions. Thank you for your participation.
iki.blockedDesc: This access is not currently available. Please contact the person who invited you to the research.
iki.consent01Text: This protocol records the temporal cadence of your typing (IKIs). This data will be used in scientific experiments and in training Artificial Intelligence models.
iki.consent02Text: Your data is processed through an irreversible cryptographic hash and will be stored on our servers for a period of 5 years for research and statistical validation purposes.
iki.consent03Text: Your participation is voluntary. In accordance with applicable data protection law (GDPR and equivalents), you have the right to request permanent deletion of your records from our database at any time through Xcorphion's support channels.
iki.consentAccept: I accept and wish to continue
iki.wpmSubtitle: Type the phrase below as fast and accurately as you can. The timer starts on the first key.
iki.wpmQuote: "The Keystroke Dynamics technique studies individual typing rhythm. Each person has a unique rhythm, pressure, and writing pattern. That is what Xcorphion is learning to understand."
iki.resumeWarningDesc: This session was previously interrupted. For best results, write without interruptions from this point forward.
iki.jitterWarningDesc: Your device shows latency fluctuations. For accurate academic results, we suggest switching to a computer with a stable connection.
iki.endRatingDesc: How do you rate the quality of this session?
iki.endGenuineTitle: Were you genuinely engaged with this research?
iki.equipmentDesc: Your device is not valid for this research. Switch to another device and try again in 5 minutes.
iki.equipmentHint: For faster access, go to xcorphion.online/conect and enter the code below:
iki.onboardingError: Error saving profile. Check your connection and try again.

finish.descDefault: Thank you for your delivery and authenticity. Register your email to receive the Session 2 link.
finish.descSent: We sent the link to {email}. You will be notified when Session 2 opens.
finish.consentDesc: By registering your email, you authorize Xcorphion to use it exclusively for communications related to this research. To confirm, type exactly:
finish.consentPasteBlocked: Pasting is not allowed. Please type manually.
finish.inviteDesc: Refer someone you believe would have something to contribute. The invitation arrives by email.

conect.inputDesc: To continue the research on this device, enter the code you received.
